   - QA phase with null/unparseable verdict now returns `success: false` with `"QA completed without a parseable verdict"` instead of silently passing
   - Exec phase now fails with `"exec produced no changes (no commits, no uncommitted work)"` when the agent session returns success but HEAD has no commits unique to it relative to `origin/main` and no uncommitted work (counted via `git rev-list --count origin/main..HEAD` so stale branches where main has advanced still report correctly)
+  - Zero-diff guard now respects custom base branches (#537): `scripts/new-feature.sh --base feature/<branch>` records the base in `branch.<name>.sequantBase`, and the guard compares against `origin/<recorded-base>` instead of hardcoded `origin/main`. Worktrees without a recorded base fall back to `origin/main` (unchanged #534 behavior), so existing worktrees and non-sequant-managed ones are unaffected
 - **Chain-mode checkpoint scoping** — `createCheckpointCommit` no longer runs `git add -A`; stages only files touched by the issue's commits relative to the chain base branch. Unrelated dirty files (e.g. `.claude/*`, `.sequant-manifest.json`) trigger a warning and skip the checkpoint instead of being swept in. Uses NUL-terminated git output (`-z`) for robust handling of paths with unicode or special characters (#528)

input.command:
git -C "/Users/tony/Projects/worktrees/feature/537-fix-orchestrator-respect-custom-base-branch-in" commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(#537): reject unsafe refs in resolveBaseRef to prevent shell injection

The resolved base ref is interpolated into a shell-executed git command
via execSync template string. The recorded value originates from the
user-supplied `--base` CLI flag and is persisted in git config — so a
value like `"main; rm -rf /"` would be spliced into the rev-list command
and executed by /bin/sh.

Threat model is limited (the user setting the flag is the one at risk —
self-harm only, not cross-user), but hygiene matters and the validation
is a one-liner. Both the current branch name and the recorded base are
now matched against a conservative refname regex; anything outside the
safe subset falls through to the origin/main fallback.

Two new unit tests cover the injection cases (recorded value with
shell metacharacters, branch name with shell metacharacters).
EOF
)"

## QA Review for Issue #537

### Size Gate

| Check | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Diff size | 407 lines (threshold: 100 default) |
| package.json changed | No |
| Security-sensitive paths | No |
| Decision | **Inline checks** (worktree, <600 lines per memory guidance) |

### AC Coverage

| AC | Source | Description | Status | Notes |
|----|--------|-------------|--------|-------|
| AC-1 | Original | `hasExecChanges` resolves correct base ref via git config | ✅ MET | `resolveBaseRef` reads `branch.<current>.sequantBase` at `phase-executor.ts:269` |
| AC-2 | Original | `new-feature.sh` records `--base` value | ✅ MET | `templates/scripts/new-feature.sh:158` (symlinked as `scripts/new-feature.sh`). Defaults to `main`. `scripts/dev/` is gitignored; tracked file is the one invoked by orchestrator |
| AC-3 | Original | Falls back to `origin/main` when no recorded base | ✅ MET | Six fallback paths in `resolveBaseRef`: rev-parse error, detached HEAD, unsafe branch, config error, empty value, unsafe value |
| AC-4 | Original | Unit test matrix (custom base + new commits, zero diff, dirty, no-config) | ✅ MET | `phase-executor.test.ts:1114-1146` — all four cases; zero-diff case verifies error string via exec-phase test at `phase-executor.test.ts:1472` |
| AC-5 | Original | `mapAgentSuccessToPhaseResult` still calls `hasExecChanges(cwd)` | ✅ MET | Signature unchanged (`phase-executor.ts:371`); base resolution encapsulated in helper |
| AC-6 | Original | Integration test or reproducer for end-to-end custom-base | ✅ MET | `phase-executor.integration.test.ts` — real bare-origin + work repo with `feature/epic` populated, `feature/537-test` branched from epic with recorded base. Sanity assertions prove HEAD is ahead of `origin/main` but zero against `origin/feature/epic` |
| AC-7 | Original | CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased]` / `Fixed` | ✅ MET | Sub-bullet under #534 `Fixed` entry, references #537 |

**Coverage:** 7/7 AC items fully met.

---

### Quality Plan Verification

N/A — Standard-tier spec; no Feature Quality Planning section in the spec comment (bug-only scope).

---

### CI Status

No PR exists yet (will be created by `/fullsolve` Phase 5).

---

### Quality Metrics

| Metric | Value | Status |
|--------|-------|--------|
| Type issues (`any`) added | 0 | ✅ OK |
| Deleted tests | 0 | ✅ OK |
| Files changed | 5 | ✅ OK |
| Lines added | +419 | — |
| Lines deleted | -10 | — |
| Build (`tsc`) | Clean | ✅ |
| Lint (`eslint --max-warnings 0`) | Clean | ✅ |
| Tests (phase-executor.*, spec-retry, batch-executor) | 150/150 | ✅ |

---

### Test Coverage Analysis

| Changed File | Tier | Has Tests? | Test File |
|--------------|------|------------|-----------|
| `src/lib/workflow/phase-executor.ts` (new `resolveBaseRef`, modified `hasExecChanges`) | Standard | ✅ Yes | `phase-executor.test.ts` + `phase-executor.integration.test.ts` |
| `templates/scripts/new-feature.sh` | Optional (shell) | Indirect | Integration test writes the same `git config` and exercises the consumer path |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Optional (docs) | — | — |

**Coverage:** 1/1 critical source file has direct tests; shell change is tested via the downstream consumer path.

---

### Code Review

**Strengths:**
- `resolveBaseRef` is small, well-commented, and has thorough JSDoc explaining intent, inputs, and fallback semantics
- Defensive programming: six explicit fallback paths to `origin/main`, all converging on a single `fallback` constant
- Proactive security hardening: added a conservative refname regex (`/^[A-Za-z0-9._/-]+$/`) to prevent shell injection via the `--base` CLI flag, with dedicated tests
- Backward compatible: pre-fix worktrees and non-sequant-managed ones fall through to `origin/main` unchanged
- Integration test uses a real bare-origin repo so `origin/main` and `origin/feature/epic` actually resolve — catches real-git semantics that mocked tests would miss
- Public API unchanged — `mapAgentSuccessToPhaseResult` callers are unaffected

**Issues:** None.

**Suggestions:** None.

---

### Test Quality Review

| Category | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Behavior vs Implementation | ✅ OK | Tests assert on return values and downstream rev-list command shape, not internals |
| Coverage Depth | ✅ OK | Matrix covers new-commits, zero-diff, dirty, fallback, shell-injection, detached HEAD, empty value |
| Mock Hygiene | ✅ OK | Unit tests mock `execSync`; integration test uses real git (no mocks) |
| Test Reliability | ✅ OK | No timers, deterministic temp-dir setup with `afterEach` cleanup |

---

### Anti-Pattern Detection

#### Dependency Audit
N/A — No `package.json` changes.

#### Code Patterns
None flagged. Pattern matches the pre-existing `hasExecChanges` implementation style (execSync with fail-open try/catch).

---

### Call-Site Review

**New exported functions detected:** 1 (`resolveBaseRef`)

| Function | Call Sites | Loop? | Conditions | AC Match |
|----------|-----------|-------|------------|----------|
| `resolveBaseRef(cwd)` | `phase-executor.ts:320` (inside `hasExecChanges`) | No | None (always called) | ✅ Matches AC-1/AC-5 |

Only internal call site. Exported with `@internal` for test access. No dead export.

---

### Execution Evidence

| Test Type | Command | Exit Code | Result |
|-----------|---------|-----------|--------|
| Shell syntax | `bash -n templates/scripts/new-feature.sh` | 0 | ✅ |
| Unit + integration tests | `npx vitest run phase-executor.*` | 0 | ✅ 111/111 |
| Build | `npm run build` | 0 | ✅ |
| Lint | `npm run lint` | 0 | ✅ 0 errors, 0 warnings |

**Evidence status:** Complete. Full end-to-end shell script invocation covered indirectly — integration test writes the same `git config` the script produces and verifies downstream semantics.

---

### Script Verification Override

**Requirement:** `/verify` before READY_FOR_MERGE
**Override:** Yes
**Justification:** Syntax-only addition to `new-feature.sh` — one `git config` write. Runtime behavior is tested end-to-end in `phase-executor.integration.test.ts`, which writes the same git config key and exercises the downstream consumer path.
**Risk Assessment:** None — the shell change has zero standalone runtime surface; its only observable effect is the git config entry, which the integration test creates directly and validates.

---

### Skill Command Verification

N/A — No skill files changed.

---

### CLI Registration Verification

N/A — No `RunOptions` or CLI option interface changes.

---

### Smoke Test

Not Required — No workflow-affecting changes. The `hasExecChanges` change is purely internal; the `new-feature.sh` change is one line of persistence.

---

### CHANGELOG Verification

|-------|--------|

| User-facing change | ✅ (orchestrator fix) |
| `[Unreleased]` entry | ✅ Present |
| Entry format | ✅ Includes (#537), under `Fixed` |

**Result:** Requirements met.

---

### Risk Assessment

- **Likely failure mode:** User creates a worktree outside of `new-feature.sh` (e.g., `git worktree add` directly) — the `branch.<name>.sequantBase` key is absent, `resolveBaseRef` falls back to `origin/main`, and zero-diff execs on a populated parent branch would pass (same pre-#537 gap for that population). This is explicitly accepted in AC-3 and documented in CHANGELOG; not a regression, just a scope boundary.
- **Not tested:** Direct end-to-end shell invocation of `new-feature.sh` (would require spawning the script and a real remote). Mitigated by the integration test writing the same config key and validating the downstream consumer path.

---

All 7 ACs met with literal verification. Build, lint, and tests are clean. Public API unchanged (AC-5). Backward compatibility preserved for pre-fix worktrees (AC-3). Proactive shell-injection hardening added with dedicated tests. Integration test uses real git to prove the end-to-end fix.

content.text:
Re-read the diff adversarially and probed three potential gaps empirically. Findings:

### Verified not-gaps

1. **Integration test uses `git init` instead of `git worktree add`** — Probed my actual worktree: `git config branch.feature/537-....sequantBase feature/epic` writes and reads back correctly. Branch-scoped config lives in `$GIT_COMMON_DIR/config` (shared across worktrees), so the integration test's plain-repo setup exercises the same code path.

2. **Branch names with `/` and `.` in git config keys** — Probed `branch.feature/test.probe/v1.2.sequantBase` round-trip: works. Git config's subsection parsing handles slashes and embedded dots.

3. **Integration test picked up by `npm test`** — `npx vitest list` shows all 7 integration cases registered under the `integration` project. Top-level `vitest run` (which is `npm test`) includes both projects.

### Real but minor limitations (acceptable scope)

4. **`scripts/new-feature.sh` not invoked end-to-end** — The one-line `git config` write is tested indirectly (integration test sets the same key directly). Full shell invocation would require a real GitHub issue + network; cost disproportionate to one literal `git config` call.

5. **`SAFE_REF` regex is stricter than git's refname rules** — Allows `[A-Za-z0-9._/-]` only. Branch names with legal-but-unusual chars (`+`, `=`, `,`) fall back to `origin/main`. Intentional — defensive against shell injection, documented in the JSDoc. Not a regression (pre-fix behavior is also `origin/main`).

6. **CI not run on PR #538 yet** — Standard; will run after this turn.

No blockers. Ready to merge on green CI.
